name: Map the service steps
description: Capture the real guest-facing service sequence, handoff boundaries, and failure-prone moments so workflow redesign starts from observed reality instead of opinion.
source_ref: core-v2: redesign workflow by observing the real service loop before assigning fixes
entry_conditions: Recurring service delay or complaint patterns suggest the real flow is poorly understood.
Leaders can observe at least one representative service window before redesigning roles or standards.
contraindications: Do not start with workshop theory alone when no live observation has been done.
Do not treat this as a paperwork exercise during an active peak collapse.
proof_of_completion: A timestamped service map exists from arrival to payment and reset.
Each handoff point has a named owner and at least one observed friction note.
Three highest-risk flow breaks are identified for intervention sequencing.
